-{"id":"bd-32svm","title":"Task: Wire checkpoint export/import --redact-secrets to shared engine","description":"## Scope
Ensure checkpoint export/import uses the canonical redaction engine instead of maintaining separate regex lists.

## Context
A prior checkpoint export/import implementation introduced `--redact-secrets` with an embedded regex list. We want a single source of truth.

## Requirements
- Preserve CLI flags and behavior (`--redact-secrets`) for compatibility.
- Internally delegate to the shared redaction engine + config.
- Ensure manifest/checksum logic isn’t impacted.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No duplicate regex list remains in checkpoint export code.
- Unit tests cover at least one end-to-end export redaction case.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:22:57.777780055Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:22:57.791525795Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32svm","depends_on_id":"bd-1sjqs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32svm","depends_on_id":"bd-20xv1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-32svm","title":"Task: Wire checkpoint export/import --redact-secrets to shared engine","description":"## Scope
Ensure checkpoint export/import uses the canonical redaction engine instead of maintaining separate regex lists.

## Context
A prior checkpoint export/import implementation introduced `--redact-secrets` with an embedded regex list. We want a single source of truth.

## Requirements
- Preserve CLI flags and behavior (`--redact-secrets`) for compatibility.
- Internally delegate to the shared redaction engine + config.
- Ensure manifest/checksum logic isn’t impacted.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No duplicate regex list remains in checkpoint export code.
- Unit tests cover at least one end-to-end export redaction case.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:22:57.777780055Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-04T04:55:09.873371222Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32svm","depends_on_id":"bd-1sjqs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32svm","depends_on_id":"bd-20xv1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-332h","title":"Task: Implement file edit detection patterns","description":"## Purpose
Extend robot/patterns.go with patterns to detect file edits from agent output.

## Detection Patterns per Agent Type
var fileWritePatterns = map[AgentType][]string{
    AgentClaude: {
        'using the edit tool',
        'using the write tool',
        'Edited file:',
        'Created file:',
        'Writing to file:',
    },
    AgentCodex: {
        'writing to file',
        'edit.*file',
        'created file',
    },
    AgentGemini: {
        'Writing:',
        'Editing:',
        'Created:',
    },
}

## Path Extraction
func ExtractEditedFiles(output string, agentType AgentType) []string

Extract file paths from patterns like:
- 'Edited file: internal/cli/send.go'
- 'Writing to file /path/to/file.go'
- Tool use JSON with file_path field

## Acceptance Criteria
- Patterns detect 90%+ of file edits
- File paths extracted accurately
- Both relative and absolute paths handled
- False positives minimized

## Technical Notes
Use regexp.MustCompile for pattern matching
Test against actual agent output samples","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:41:28.921638062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:01:31.773414528Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T10:01:31.773257222Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-332h","depends_on_id":"bd-dz3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-334vl","title":"E2E Tests: webhook/notification redaction","description":"## Goal
Verify redaction is applied to webhook payloads end-to-end.

## Scenario
- Configure a local test webhook endpoint (`httptest` server) that records request bodies.
- Produce an event/notification payload containing a synthetic secret marker.
- Verify:
  - request body does **not** contain the plaintext marker
  - it contains redacted placeholders + redaction summary fields

## Test Harness + Logging
- Implement as Go E2E tests under `e2e/` with the existing build tag (`//go:build e2e`).
- Use `e2e.TestLogger` (writes to `E2E_LOG_DIR` or `/tmp/ntm-e2e-logs`).
- Log:
  - webhook URL
  - captured raw request body (must already be redacted)
  - parsed JSON payload (pretty printed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Default unit suite remains green: `go test ./...`.
- E2E suite passes: `go test -tags e2e ./e2e`.
- Uses only synthetic fixtures.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:49:25.997396174Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T04:12:50.060951820Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-334vl","depends_on_id":"bd-143vt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-334vl","depends_on_id":"bd-9rb7b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3o9b0","title":"Implement bead assignment integration","description":"Implement bead assignment integration with --bead flag.

## Feature
When --bead=<ID> is provided, automatically send bead assignment prompt after restart.

## Prompt Template
Read AGENTS.md, register with Agent Mail. Work on: <BEAD_ID> - <DESC>.
Use br show <BEAD_ID> for details. Use ultrathink.

## Implementation
1. Fetch bead info via br show --json
2. Format prompt with bead ID and description
3. Send prompt to pane after agent is ready
4. Return bead_assigned and prompt_sent in response

## Alternative: --prompt flag
Allow custom prompt instead of bead template for flexibility.

## Error Handling
- If bead ID doesn't exist: return error before restart
- If prompt send fails: note in response but still report restart success","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:25.108162363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:34:29.635186608Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:34:29.635053721Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3o9b0","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oeov","title":"EPIC: REST API v1 (Full CLI/Robot Parity)","description":"## Summary\nExpose the full NTM feature surface via a REST API that mirrors robot mode responses and supports idempotency, jobs, and OpenAPI documentation.\n\n## Scope\n- API server scaffolding with chi router, middleware, error envelope, request IDs.\n- Core resources: system, sessions, panes, agents, output tooling, palette/history, checkpoints, metrics, context, git.\n- Tool bridges: Agent Mail + reservations, Beads/BV, CASS/CM, Scanner, Accounts, Safety, Pipelines.\n- OpenAPI 3.1 spec generation + Swagger UI at /docs.\n- Consistent envelopes + error codes aligned with robot mode.\n\n## Success Criteria\n- Every CLI/robot feature has a REST endpoint.\n- OpenAPI spec is generated from kernel and stays in sync.\n- Idempotent POSTs support Idempotency-Key.\n- Long‑running ops return job handles.\n","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:48:20.934770050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T23:18:27.607825603Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-12vyy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-15amw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-1lxvl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-204ic","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-2q9v1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-2vzaq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-30zp4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-38p9u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-3cw6i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-3fqg9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-3hsvc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-3iomn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-6z2bf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oeov","depends_on_id":"bd-f8ikz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ol6","title":"E2E Tests: Full task assignment workflow","description":"E2E Tests for Full task assignment workflow.

## Test Scenarios

1. Basic assignment flow
   - Create test beads with bv
   - Spawn agents
   - Run ntm assign
   - Verify tasks distributed
   - Verify file reservations created

2. Priority-based assignment
   - Create P1, P2, P3 tasks
   - Run assignment
   - Verify P1 tasks assigned first
   - Verify P3 tasks queued

3. Dependency respect
   - Create tasks with dependencies
   - Run assignment
   - Verify blockers assigned before blocked

4. Interactive mode
   - Run ntm assign --interactive
   - Simulate user selections
   - Verify chosen assignments applied

## Detailed Logging
```
[E2E-ASSIGN] Loading tasks from bv: found N
[E2E-ASSIGN] Assigning TASK_ID to AGENT (priority=N)
[E2E-ASSIGN] Reserved files for AGENT: FILES
[E2E-ASSIGN] Assignment complete: N tasks to N agents
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 4 test scenarios implemented
2. Basic flow creates file reservations
3. P1 tasks assigned before P2/P3
4. Dependencies respected (blockers first)
5. Interactive mode responds to simulated input
6. Detailed logging with [E2E-ASSIGN] prefix
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. E2E tests verify full task assignment
2. BV-driven assignment tested
3. Assignment persists correctly
4. Assignment notifications sent
5. Test cleanup removes assignments
6. Test duration under 5 minutes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:15:21.439072364Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T03:34:36.568982216Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T03:34:36.568475522Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ol6","depends_on_id":"bd-27ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ol6","depends_on_id":"bd-9tbv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3pa8p","title":"Task: Unit tests for robot verbosity/short-keys/pagination","description":"## Overview
Add unit tests for robot output ergonomics features: verbosity profiles, short‑key mappings, and pagination/limits.

## Tests
- `--robot-verbosity=full|terse` toggles optional fields as specified
- Short‑key map produces stable, reversible keys (if enabled)
- Pagination returns deterministic `next_cursor` + correct `count`/`total` behavior
- Large payloads respect limits without breaking envelope

## Logging
Use `t.Logf` with structured prefixes:
- `t.Logf("ROBOT_TEST: verbosity=%s size=%d", mode, size)`
- `t.Logf("ROBOT_TEST: shortkeys=%t fields=%v", enabled, keys)`
- `t.Logf("ROBOT_TEST: page limit=%d next=%s", limit, next)`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic fixtures for each verbosity level
- Pagination behavior covered for edge cases (empty, exact limit, overflow)
- Tests run without external dependencies","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:24:13.235689238Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:56:02.061959482Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:56:02.061611244Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pa8p","depends_on_id":"bd-20ong","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pa8p","depends_on_id":"bd-2dovu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pa8p","depends_on_id":"bd-2tb5i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pa8p","depends_on_id":"bd-4uecx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pa8p","depends_on_id":"bd-9l7cp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3pbe","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Audit Log System","description":"## Overview
Implement an immutable, structured audit log that records all significant NTM actions, enabling debugging, compliance, and operational insights.

## Problem Statement
NTM lacks comprehensive activity tracking. Users can't:
- Debug what happened in a session hours ago
- Prove compliance for regulated environments
- Understand why something failed
- Track who/what made changes
- Reconstruct incident timelines

## Key Features
1. Immutable append-only log file per session
2. Structured JSON format for machine parsing
3. Human-readable summary export
4. Query commands: ntm audit search, ntm audit show
5. Retention policies with automatic cleanup
6. Integration with enterprise logging (syslog, CloudWatch)

## Log Entry Types
- command: CLI commands executed
- spawn: Agent creation events
- send: Prompts sent to agents
- response: Agent responses (summarized)
- error: Errors and warnings
- state_change: Agent state transitions
- config_change: Configuration modifications

## Log Format (JSONL)
{
  "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "session_id": "myproject",
  "event_type": "send",
  "actor": "user",
  "target": "myproject__cc_1",
  "payload": {"prompt_preview": "Fix the auth bug...", "prompt_length": 500},
  "metadata": {"correlation_id": "abc123"}
}

## CLI Commands
- ntm audit show <session> - view session audit log
- ntm audit search <query> - search across all logs
- ntm audit export <session> --format=json|csv|html
- ntm audit tail - live follow of current session

## Success Criteria
- All significant actions logged with timestamps
- Logs are tamper-evident (checksums)
- Can reconstruct session timeline from log
- Query tools work efficiently
- Compliant with common audit requirements

## Files Likely Affected
- internal/audit/logger.go (new - audit logging)
- internal/audit/query.go (new - search/filter)
- internal/audit/export.go (new - export formats)
- internal/cli/audit.go (new - CLI commands)","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:32:46.019835408Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:48:15.130062117Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3pnw7","title":"Subtask: Implement Agent-Specific Pattern Definitions","description":"## Subtask: Implement Agent-Specific Pattern Definitions

### Purpose
Define regex patterns and string matchers for each agent type. This is the "knowledge base" of how each agent expresses its state.

### File to Create
`internal/agent/patterns.go`

### Pattern Categories

For each agent type, we need patterns for:
1. **Rate Limit Detection** - Agent hit usage cap
2. **Context/Usage Indicators** - How much capacity remains
3. **Working Indicators** - Agent actively producing output
4. **Idle Indicators** - Agent waiting for input
5. **Error Indicators** - Something went wrong

### Claude Code (cc) Patterns

```go
package agent

import "regexp"

// Claude Code patterns
var (
    // Rate limit - agent cannot continue
    ccRateLimitPatterns = []string{
        "You.ve hit your limit",
        "Rate limit exceeded", 
        "Please wait",
        "try again later",
        "too many requests",
        "usage limit",
    }
    
    // Context warnings - running low
    ccContextWarnings = []string{
        "This conversation is getting long",
        "context limit",
        "running out of context",
        "conversation.*long",
    }
    
    // Working - actively producing (DO NOT INTERRUPT)
    ccWorkingPatterns = []string{
        "```",           // Code block delimiter
        "Writing to ",
        "Created ",
        "Modified ",
        "Deleted ",
        "Reading ",
        "Searching ",
        "Running ",
        "Executing ",
        "Installing ",
        "Thinking",
        "Processing",
        "Analyzing",
    }
    
    // Idle - waiting for input (safe to restart)
    ccIdlePatterns = []*regexp.Regexp{
        regexp.MustCompile(`>\s*$`),           // Prompt
        regexp.MustCompile(`Human:\s*$`),      // Conversation mode
        regexp.MustCompile(`waiting for input`),
    }
    
    // Header detection - confirms this is Claude Code
    ccHeaderPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`Opus \d+\.\d+.*Claude`)
)
```

### Codex CLI (cod) Patterns

```go
var (
    // CRITICAL: Codex shows explicit context percentage!
    // "47% context left · ? for shortcuts"
    codContextPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`(\d+)%\s*context\s*left`)
    
    // Token usage after response
    // "Token usage: total=219,582 input=206,150 ..."
    codTokenPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`Token usage:\s*total=(\d[\d,]*)`)
    
    // Rate limit
    codRateLimitPatterns = []string{
        "You.ve reached your usage limit",
        "Rate limit exceeded",
        "quota exceeded",
        "capacity",
    }
    
    // Working indicators
    codWorkingPatterns = []string{
        "```",
        "Editing ",
        "Creating ",
        "Writing ",
        "Running ",
        "$ ",  // Shell command output
    }
    
    // Idle
    codIdlePatterns = []*regexp.Regexp{
        regexp.MustCompile(`>\s*$`),
        regexp.MustCompile(`\?\s*for shortcuts`), // Prompt line
    }
    
    // Header detection
    codHeaderPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`codex|openai`)
)
```

### Gemini CLI (gmi) Patterns

```go
var (
    // Memory usage (less precise than Codex)
    // "gemini-3-pro-preview /model | 396.8 MB"
    gmiMemoryPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`(\d+\.?\d*)\s*MB`)
    
    // YOLO mode indicator
    gmiYoloPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`YOLO mode:\s*(ON|OFF)`)
    
    // Rate limit (less explicit - heuristic)
    gmiRateLimitPatterns = []string{
        "error",
        "failed",
        "quota",
        "limit reached",
        "try again",
    }
    
    // Working indicators
    gmiWorkingPatterns = []string{
        "```",
        "Creating ",
        "Writing ",
        "Executing ",
    }
    
    // GOTCHA: Shell mode triggered by "!"
    gmiShellModePattern = regexp.MustCompile(`^!\s*`)
    
    // Header detection
    gmiHeaderPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`gemini.*preview|gemini-\d`)
)
```

### Pattern Matching Helpers

```go
// matchAny returns true if text matches any pattern
func matchAny(text string, patterns []string) bool {
    textLower := strings.ToLower(text)
    for _, p := range patterns {
        if strings.Contains(textLower, strings.ToLower(p)) {
            return true
        }
    }
    return false
}

// matchAnyRegex returns true if text matches any regex
func matchAnyRegex(text string, patterns []*regexp.Regexp) bool {
    for _, p := range patterns {
        if p.MatchString(text) {
            return true
        }
    }
    return false
}

// extractFloat extracts first float from regex match
func extractFloat(pattern *regexp.Regexp, text string) *float64 {
    match := pattern.FindStringSubmatch(text)
    if len(match) < 2 {
        return nil
    }
    val, err := strconv.ParseFloat(match[1], 64)
    if err != nil {
        return nil
    }
    return &val
}
```

### Why These Specific Patterns?

1. **Rate limit patterns are broad** - False positives here are OK (we just wait). False negatives are costly (we might interrupt).

2. **Working patterns include code blocks** - The "```" delimiter is the most reliable indicator of active output.

3. **Codex context pattern is most valuable** - It is the ONLY agent that gives explicit percentage. We extract this precisely.

4. **Gemini patterns are heuristic** - Gemini is less explicit, so we use softer matching and lower confidence.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All patterns compile without regex errors
- [ ] Patterns are case-insensitive where appropriate
- [ ] Helper functions handle edge cases
- [ ] Patterns documented with examples","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-20T21:58:37.263265726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T01:39:35.629175929Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T01:39:35.628338021Z","close_reason":"Implemented agent-specific patterns for cc, cod, gmi agents including rate limit, working, idle, error patterns, and helper functions for pattern matching. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pnw7","depends_on_id":"bd-120q6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pnw7","depends_on_id":"bd-2iw6e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Added comprehensive tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h1fb","depends_on_id":"bd-38kx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-h3ha","title":"Add ntm send --randomize - randomize order of individualized prompts to avoid thundering herd on same files","description":"## Overview
Add --randomize flag to ntm send to randomize the order of individualized prompts, avoiding thundering herd problems when multiple agents start working on overlapping files.

## Problem
When sending individualized prompts (e.g., each agent gets a different bead), agents often start simultaneously on related files. This causes:
- Git conflicts when multiple agents edit the same area
- File reservation conflicts in Agent Mail
- Wasted work from merge conflicts

## Solution
`ntm send myproject --randomize` shuffles the order of prompts before sending.

## Implementation
```go
func randomizePrompts(prompts []Prompt) []Prompt {
    shuffled := make([]Prompt, len(prompts))
    copy(shuffled, prompts)
    rand.Shuffle(len(shuffled), func(i, j int) {
        shuffled[i], shuffled[j] = shuffled[j], shuffled[i]
    })
    return shuffled
}
```

## CLI Examples
```bash
# Randomize prompt order
ntm send myproject --randomize

# Combine with batch
ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt --randomize

# Combine with delay for extra staggering  
ntm send myproject --randomize --delay 2s
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Prompts shuffled before sending
- [ ] Deterministic seed option for reproducibility (--seed=N)
- [ ] Works with --batch and --distribute
- [ ] Logging shows shuffle order
- [ ] No effect if only one prompt

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Shuffle produces different order
- Unit test: Seed produces reproducible order
- E2E test: Randomized batch send","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-h5oyj","title":"Task: Add ntm audit CLI commands","description":"## Overview
Implement CLI commands for interacting with audit logs.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/cli/audit.go:
   - ntm audit show <session> - display session audit log
   - ntm audit search <query> - search across all logs
   - ntm audit tail [session] - live follow of audit log
   - ntm audit export <session> - export to file
   - ntm audit verify <session> - verify log integrity

2. ntm audit show:
   - Display formatted audit entries
   - Color-coded by event type
   - Pagination for long logs
   - Options: --since, --until, --type, --limit

3. ntm audit search:
   - Full-text search across all sessions
   - Display matching entries with context
   - Options: --sessions, --days, --type

4. ntm audit tail:
   - Live follow like 'tail -f'
   - Filter by event type
   - Format: timestamp | type | target | summary

5. ntm audit export:
   - Formats: json, csv, html
   - Include summary statistics
   - Optionally include full payloads

6. ntm audit verify:
   - Check checksum chain integrity
   - Report any gaps or modifications
   - Output: PASS or FAIL with details

## Implementation Notes
- Use cobra subcommands
- Consistent output formatting
- Support --json flag for machine output

## Acceptance Criteria
- All commands work correctly
- Output is readable and useful
- Verification detects tampering
- Unit tests for CLI commands","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:33:26.562442178Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:33:58.863853814Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h5oyj","depends_on_id":"bd-22cf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-h5oyj","depends_on_id":"bd-3pbe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-h8062","title":"Test: Mock tmux client for testing","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end integration test script for the complete swarm orchestration workflow, from scanning to agent launch to limit detection and respawn.

## Test Script Structure

```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# scripts/test_swarm_e2e.sh
# End-to-end test for weighted multi-project agent swarm orchestration
#
# Prerequisites:
# - ntm binary built and in PATH
# - tmux installed
# - br (beads_rust) installed
# - Test project directories set up

set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# Configuration
# ============================================================================
TEST_DIR="${TEST_DIR:-/tmp/ntm_e2e_test_$$}"
LOG_FILE="${TEST_DIR}/e2e_test.log"
VERBOSE="${VERBOSE:-1}"

# Colors for output
RED="\033[0;31m"
GREEN="\033[0;32m"
YELLOW="\033[1;33m"
NC="\033[0m" # No Color

# ============================================================================
# Logging Functions
# ============================================================================
log() {
    local level=$1
    shift
    local msg="$*"
    local timestamp=$(date "+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%3N")
    echo -e "[$timestamp] [$level] $msg" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_info()  { log "INFO " "$@"; }
log_warn()  { log "WARN " "${YELLOW}$*${NC}"; }
log_error() { log "ERROR" "${RED}$*${NC}"; }
log_pass()  { log "PASS " "${GREEN}$*${NC}"; }
log_fail()  { log "FAIL " "${RED}$*${NC}"; }

log_section() {
    echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    echo "============================================================" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    echo "  $1" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    echo "============================================================" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

# ============================================================================
# Setup Functions
# ============================================================================
setup_test_environment() {
    log_section "Setting up test environment"
    
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR"
    log_info "Test directory: $TEST_DIR"
    log_info "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
    
    # Create test projects with different bead counts
    log_info "Creating test projects..."
    
    # Tier 1 project (400+ beads)
    create_test_project "tier1-project" 450
    
    # Tier 2 project (100-399 beads)
    create_test_project "tier2-project" 200
    
    # Tier 3 project (<100 beads)
    create_test_project "tier3-project" 50
    
    # Project without beads (should be skipped)
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/no-beads-project"
    log_info "Created no-beads-project (should be skipped)"
    
    log_pass "Test environment setup complete"
}

create_test_project() {
    local name=$1
    local bead_count=$2
    local project_dir="$TEST_DIR/$name"
    
    mkdir -p "$project_dir/.beads"
    
    # Create mock beads
    for i in $(seq 1 $bead_count); do
        echo "{\"id\":\"bd-$i\",\"status\":\"open\",\"title\":\"Test bead $i\"}" >> "$project_dir/.beads/issues.jsonl"
    done
    
    log_info "Created $name with $bead_count beads"
}

# ============================================================================
# Test Cases
# ============================================================================
test_dry_run() {
    log_section "Test: Dry Run Mode"
    
    log_info "Running: ntm swarm --scan-dir $TEST_DIR --dry-run"
    
    local output
    output=$(ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --dry-run 2>&1) || {
        log_fail "Dry run command failed"
        log_error "Output: $output"
        return 1
    }
    
    log_info "Dry run output:"
    echo "$output" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    
    # Verify expected output
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "tier1-project"; then
        log_pass "Tier 1 project detected"
    else
        log_fail "Tier 1 project NOT detected"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "tier2-project"; then
        log_pass "Tier 2 project detected"
    else
        log_fail "Tier 2 project NOT detected"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "no-beads-project"; then
        log_fail "No-beads project should NOT appear"
        return 1
    else
        log_pass "No-beads project correctly excluded"
    fi
    
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "Dry-run complete"; then
        log_pass "Dry run completed without execution"
    else
        log_fail "Dry run indicator not found"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Dry run test PASSED"
}

test_plan_output() {
    log_section "Test: Plan JSON Output"
    
    local plan_file="$TEST_DIR/swarm_plan.json"
    
    log_info "Running: ntm swarm --scan-dir $TEST_DIR --dry-run --output $plan_file"
    
    ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --dry-run --output "$plan_file" 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    
    if [[ \! -f "$plan_file" ]]; then
        log_fail "Plan file not created"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_info "Plan file created: $plan_file"
    log_info "Plan contents:"
    cat "$plan_file" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    
    # Validate JSON structure
    if \! jq -e . "$plan_file" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_fail "Plan file is not valid JSON"
        return 1
    fi
    log_pass "Plan file is valid JSON"
    
    # Check required fields
    local total_agents
    total_agents=$(jq -r ".total_agents" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Total agents in plan: $total_agents"
    
    if [[ "$total_agents" -gt 0 ]]; then
        log_pass "Plan contains agent allocations"
    else
        log_fail "Plan has no agent allocations"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Plan output test PASSED"
}

test_session_creation() {
    log_section "Test: Session Creation (requires tmux)"
    
    # Skip if tmux not available
    if \! command -v tmux &> /dev/null; then
        log_warn "tmux not available, skipping session creation test"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Clean up any existing test sessions
    tmux kill-session -t "cc_agents_1" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    log_info "Running swarm with single session for test..."
    log_info "ntm swarm --scan-dir $TEST_DIR --sessions-per-type 1 --dry-run"
    
    # For actual execution (commented out for safety):
    # ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --sessions-per-type 1
    
    # Verify sessions would be created
    local plan_file="$TEST_DIR/session_test_plan.json"
    ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --sessions-per-type 1 --dry-run --output "$plan_file"
    
    local session_count
    session_count=$(jq -r ".sessions | length" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Sessions in plan: $session_count"
    
    if [[ "$session_count" -ge 3 ]]; then
        log_pass "Plan includes expected session count (3 types)"
    else
        log_fail "Unexpected session count: $session_count"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check session naming
    local cc_session
    cc_session=$(jq -r ".sessions[] | select(.agent_type==\"cc\") | .name" "$plan_file" | head -1)
    log_info "CC session name: $cc_session"
    
    if [[ "$cc_session" == "cc_agents_1" ]]; then
        log_pass "Session naming is correct"
    else
        log_fail "Session naming mismatch: expected cc_agents_1, got $cc_session"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Session creation test PASSED"
}

test_allocation_tiers() {
    log_section "Test: Allocation Tier Calculations"
    
    local plan_file="$TEST_DIR/tier_test_plan.json"
    ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --dry-run --output "$plan_file"
    
    # Check tier 1 allocation
    local tier1_cc
    tier1_cc=$(jq -r ".allocations[] | select(.project.name==\"tier1-project\") | .cc_agents" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Tier 1 (450 beads) CC allocation: $tier1_cc"
    
    # Check tier 2 allocation  
    local tier2_cc
    tier2_cc=$(jq -r ".allocations[] | select(.project.name==\"tier2-project\") | .cc_agents" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Tier 2 (200 beads) CC allocation: $tier2_cc"
    
    # Check tier 3 allocation
    local tier3_cc
    tier3_cc=$(jq -r ".allocations[] | select(.project.name==\"tier3-project\") | .cc_agents" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Tier 3 (50 beads) CC allocation: $tier3_cc"
    
    # Verify tier hierarchy
    if [[ "$tier1_cc" -gt "$tier2_cc" ]] && [[ "$tier2_cc" -ge "$tier3_cc" ]]; then
        log_pass "Tier allocation hierarchy is correct"
    else
        log_fail "Tier allocation hierarchy violated: T1=$tier1_cc, T2=$tier2_cc, T3=$tier3_cc"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Allocation tier test PASSED"
}

# ============================================================================
# Cleanup
# ============================================================================
cleanup() {
    log_section "Cleanup"
    
    # Kill any test tmux sessions
    tmux kill-session -t "cc_agents_1" 2>/dev/null || true
    tmux kill-session -t "cod_agents_1" 2>/dev/null || true
    tmux kill-session -t "gmi_agents_1" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    if [[ "${KEEP_TEST_DIR:-0}" == "0" ]]; then
        log_info "Removing test directory: $TEST_DIR"
        rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
    else
        log_info "Keeping test directory: $TEST_DIR"
    fi
    
    log_info "Cleanup complete"
}

# ============================================================================
# Main
# ============================================================================
main() {
    log_section "NTM Swarm E2E Test Suite"
    log_info "Starting at $(date)"
    log_info "Test directory: $TEST_DIR"
    
    trap cleanup EXIT
    
    local failed=0
    
    setup_test_environment
    
    test_dry_run || ((failed++))
    test_plan_output || ((failed++))
    test_allocation_tiers || ((failed++))
    test_session_creation || ((failed++))
    
    log_section "Test Summary"
    if [[ $failed -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_pass "All tests PASSED"
        exit 0
    else
        log_fail "$failed test(s) FAILED"
        exit 1
    fi
}

main "$@"
```

package swarm_test

)

func TestE2E_FullSwarmLifecycle(t *testing.T) {

    }
    
    t.Log("=== E2E Full Swarm Lifecycle Test ===")
    
    // Setup
    testDir := t.TempDir()
    t.Logf("Test directory: %s", testDir)
    
    // ... test implementation
}

    local session_count
    session_count=$(jq -r ".sessions | length" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Sessions in plan: $session_count"
    
    if [[ "$session_count" -ge 3 ]]; then
        log_pass "Plan includes expected session count (3 types)"
    else
        log_fail "Unexpected session count: $session_count"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check session naming
    local cc_session
    cc_session=$(jq -r ".sessions[] | select(.agent_type==\"cc\") | .name" "$plan_file" | head -1)
    log_info "CC session name: $cc_session"
    
    if [[ "$cc_session" == "cc_agents_1" ]]; then
        log_pass "Session naming is correct"
    else
        log_fail "Session naming mismatch: expected cc_agents_1, got $cc_session"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Session creation test PASSED"
}

test_allocation_tiers() {
    log_section "Test: Allocation Tier Calculations"
    
    local plan_file="$TEST_DIR/tier_test_plan.json"
    ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --dry-run --output "$plan_file"
    
    # Check tier 1 allocation
    local tier1_cc
    tier1_cc=$(jq -r ".allocations[] | select(.project.name==\"tier1-project\") | .cc_agents" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Tier 1 (450 beads) CC allocation: $tier1_cc"
    
    # Check tier 2 allocation  
    local tier2_cc
    tier2_cc=$(jq -r ".allocations[] | select(.project.name==\"tier2-project\") | .cc_agents" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Tier 2 (200 beads) CC allocation: $tier2_cc"
    
    # Check tier 3 allocation
    local tier3_cc
    tier3_cc=$(jq -r ".allocations[] | select(.project.name==\"tier3-project\") | .cc_agents" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Tier 3 (50 beads) CC allocation: $tier3_cc"
    
    # Verify tier hierarchy
    if [[ "$tier1_cc" -gt "$tier2_cc" ]] && [[ "$tier2_cc" -ge "$tier3_cc" ]]; then
        log_pass "Tier allocation hierarchy is correct"
    else
        log_fail "Tier allocation hierarchy violated: T1=$tier1_cc, T2=$tier2_cc, T3=$tier3_cc"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Allocation tier test PASSED"
}

# ============================================================================
# Cleanup
# ============================================================================
cleanup() {
    log_section "Cleanup"
    
    # Kill any test tmux sessions
    tmux kill-session -t "cc_agents_1" 2>/dev/null || true
    tmux kill-session -t "cod_agents_1" 2>/dev/null || true
    tmux kill-session -t "gmi_agents_1" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    if [[ "${KEEP_TEST_DIR:-0}" == "0" ]]; then
        log_info "Removing test directory: $TEST_DIR"
        rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
    else
        log_info "Keeping test directory: $TEST_DIR"
    fi
    
    log_info "Cleanup complete"
}

# ============================================================================
# Main
# ============================================================================
main() {
    log_section "NTM Swarm E2E Test Suite"
    log_info "Starting at $(date)"
    log_info "Test directory: $TEST_DIR"
    
    trap cleanup EXIT
    
    local failed=0
    
    setup_test_environment
    
    test_dry_run || ((failed++))
    test_plan_output || ((failed++))
    test_allocation_tiers || ((failed++))
    test_session_creation || ((failed++))
    
    log_section "Test Summary"
    if [[ $failed -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_pass "All tests PASSED"
        exit 0
    else
        log_fail "$failed test(s) FAILED"
        exit 1
    fi
}

main "$@"
```

func TestE2E_FullSwarmLifecycle(t *testing.T) {

        t.Skip("Skipping E2E test (set NTM_E2E=1 to run)")
    }
    
    t.Log("=== E2E Full Swarm Lifecycle Test ===")
    
    // Setup
    testDir := t.TempDir()
    t.Logf("Test directory: %s", testDir)
    
    // ... test implementation
}

## Logging Requirements
- Timestamped log entries
- Color-coded output for pass/fail
- Section headers for test organization
- Full command output captured
- JSON validation logging
- Summary with pass/fail counts

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test dry-run mode completely
- [ ] Test JSON plan output
- [ ] Test tier allocation calculations
- [ ] Test session naming conventions
- [ ] Test project discovery/exclusion
- [ ] Cleanup tmux sessions after test
- [ ] Configurable test directory
- [ ] Verbose logging option
- [ ] Exit codes for CI integration
